desc:
As the shaft continues its vertical journey through the underground, a tunnel
suddenly opens to the north. A ledge has been cut into the wall allowing easy
access to the tunnel. From the ledge wooden ladders leave both up through the
shaft and further down into the mines. Thick ropes, emerging from the darkness
above, continue down towards the bottom of the shaft.
name: Mine Shaft

desc:
There is no ceiling in this tunnel, instead the dark walls leap upward into a
broad shaft looming above the tunnel. Thick ropes dangle in the darkness from
the shaft above. Sturdy wooden ladders mounted on the walls by the use of iron
hoops ascend into the shaft above. The worn floor is smooth to the touch, worn
down by busy feet labouring in the mine.
name: Beneath a Mine Shaft

desc:
The old tunnel leads onward through the underground, connecting the various
parts of the dwarven mine. The worn floor is made of hard mountain rock, and any
sound made seems to echo through the tunnel bouncing between the walls. A faint
draft sweeps through the tunnel, indicating that some sort of exit must be near.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
As a bearer of the gift of Aule the tunnel wanders onward heedless of the slowly
increasing slope. The surrounding walls follow the descent without curving the
least. The well-crafted tunnel is used mainly for transportation of ore and
other goods about the mine. A few holes near a darker spot on one of the walls
testify to the fruitless attempts to find iron in this tunnel.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
Beneath a broad ceiling the old tunnel continues through the core of the Blue
Mountains. The carved halls and tunnels built by dwarven hands seems to go on
forever. Deep inside the mountains the dwarves have dwelt for ages now, and with
each change of season the mines expand and the glorious dwarven carving skill is
preserved.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
This mine was apparently created during the first attempts to extract the ore
from the mountain. The surrounding walls show the signs of these efforts.
Precise and careful was the work of the dwarves here, as almost all of the ore
was removed and the mine is near empty.
name: Old Mine

desc:
Layers of dust have settled in the old mine. Once the sound of splitting stone
filled the tunnels as dwarves extracted the iron ore from the mountain. Now the
mine is abandoned, left to spiders who spin their webs in every corner. In the
distance can be heard the sound of metal tools thrust against the hard mountain
rock.
name: Abandoned Mine

desc:
This unfinished tunnel seems to have been abandoned long ago. Bleak cobwebs form
light bridges spanning across the unfinished stone walls for the marching army
of dust that covers the tunnel. There are many old mining tools scattered about
here, but all are cracked and pitted by the weight of many centuries of disuse.
It would appear that the tunnel was abandoned in a hurry.
name: Old Mine

desc:
The solemn decrepitude of the worn tunnel was born with the passing of the mine
workers. In times long gone the tunnel was mined by dwarven craftsmen, but now
that the ore has been extracted and only empty walls are left the tunnel has
been abandoned. The walls and ceiling are held in place by thick beams of wood
to prevent a cave-in in what seems a fairly unstable region of the mine.
name: Abandoned Mine

desc:
Your journey through the dwarven mines has taken you to a little used area. This
part of the mine must have been one of the first excavations and has probably
been abandoned for a long time. It would appear that the departing of the
dwarves and the depletion of ore were closely related events. This tunnel is not
used for transportation either, as dwarven interest has found new roots
elsewhere.
name: Abandoned Mine

desc:
The tunnel reveals many signs of careless mining. Although great persistence
drove the dwarves to seek wealth in the mountain, they must have been somewhat
disappointed with the obvious lack of ore in this region. The surrounding walls
look unstable and ready to cave in at any moment. The ceiling however looks
quite strong.
name: Worn Tunnel

desc:
Straight walls of hard rock stand like solemn guardians supporting a broad
ceiling that is full of scars from digging done long ago. The old tunnel appears
to have been dug in the time when the dwarves first began mining the Blue
Mountains. There are no signs of ore in the tunnel and it would seem that it has
never been mined actively, but it still has its function in the mine.
Apparently, it is used regularly for transportation as ore is brought out and
supplies and tools return the other way.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
A passage branches off from the main tunnel to the north, passing under a stone
arch which leaps across the ceiling with a broad curve and then leans on sturdy
stone pillars with many intricate carvings. The floor is perfectly smooth here,
yet not entirely due to extensive labour, but apparently also to being worn down
by busy feet. Except for the carved pillars under the arch the tunnel is quite
ordinary.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel digging its way through the depths of the mountain passes through
dark walls of hard rock carved with the precision only the Dwarves possess. The
tunnel is otherwise quite plain. No ornamentations or expensive decorations can
be seen. Most likely, this tunnel was constructed a long time ago as a part of
the first explorations into the mountain conducted by Thorin and Thrain.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
The old tunnel plunges through the underground as though it has inherited the
obduracy of its dwarven creators. There is little ore in this part of the mine,
yet the dwarves apparently continued digging through this region in hope of
finding riches further on.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
In a dusty recess of the elaborate dwarven tunnel-systems beneath the vast bulk
of the Blue Mountains lies a small corridor in the mountainside. Carved
meticulously, with skills and determination given the Dwarves by Aule, the
corridor is a proud display of dwarven history told by many murals on the stone
walls. Between the murals are arrayed various types of axes securely mounted
into the wall. A strong oaken door rests comfortably fitting into a stone-set
door-frame to the north.
name: Corridor

desc:
A curving stone ceiling looms above the tunnel. The hard rock has been polished
by skilful hands and appears perfectly smooth, yet on the northern wall fine
lines carved into the stone depict some sort of mural. The cold stone floor is
even and without any cracks or other damage. This tunnel was undoubtedly carved
with the intention that it should last far beyond the lifespan of its creator.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
The craftsmanship of the Dwarves is fully revealed as the faint light from oil
lanterns hanging from the ceiling is reflected in the smooth surface of the
tunnel walls. The ceiling in the dry tunnel is supported only by the walls; the
weight of the mountain alone holds everything in place. In ages long gone, this
tunnel had probably been a part of the first dwarven explorations into the
depths of the Blue Mountains.
name: Old Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
Dark walls of hard mountain rock make a solid foundation for the broad ceiling
that spans across the tunnel. The even floor is tinged with a blue colour that
seems to originate in the depths of the mountain itself. The immaculate
surroundings have been carved with the utmost precision that can only be found
in the work of Dwarves. A niche is carved into the west wall.
name: Mine Tunnel

desc:
The dwarves busy themselves in the labour of the mine as you can hear by the
distant sounds of metal upon hard rock passing through the air. Under the Blue
Mountains lie vast treasures which the dwarves mine with a determination beyond
that of men and elves. This tunnel seems to have been mined dry without much
gain, but it is skilfully crafted with strong beams of wood supporting the
ceiling and the walls.
name: Mine Tunnel

desc:
The ageing of this mine must have started a long time ago when the dwarves made
their first explorations into the depths of the mountain. Judging by the marks
along the sides of the tunnel, heavy mining took place here once, and at that
time the mine probably played an active part in the consolidation of wealth
among the dwarves. Now it appears to be more or less abandoned and it is only
seldom that any dwarf might pass here when duties require that goods are brought
to the outer parts of the mines.
name: Old Mine

desc:
A curve in the stone stretches the scars of dwarven pickaxes across the ceiling
of the tunnel. This area was hewed through the core of the Blue Mountains by the
hands of busy dwarves searching for ore. Now the tunnel is abandoned and closer
inspection reveals that it was actually never quite finished.
name: Old Mine

desc:
The skill of dwarven pickaxes tells on the surrounding walls the story of the
dwarven effort to mine the underground of the Blue Mountains. Yet the effort
appears to have stopped here. On the floor of the almost completed tunnel lie
several discarded pickaxes, and broken rocks produced by the mining are
scattered along the walls.
name: Old Mine

desc:
Between huge stones towering around you like giants runs a narrow fissure in the
mountain. The walls are very close, and moving through the fissure would shift
between crawling on all fours and climbing up steep rocks. This fissure is
natural and there are no signs that dwarves were ever active in this area.
name: Twisting Fissure

desc:
A narrow fissure between towering boulders proceeds through the underground.
Darkness reigns here, feeding on any source of light that may pass. Grumbling
and bickering, the mountain narrows the fissure making passage hard and
troublesome.
name: Dark Fissure

desc:
In the inner depths of the Blue Mountains your most trusty follower is the
rumbling echo carrying the sound of every footstep through the turning tunnels.
The tunnels here are natural and worn with the passing of time. Steep walls of
flint, cracked and torn with sharp spikes, rise on every side of you. The black
passage reveals no apparent signs of life.
name: Broken Passage

desc:
Mouldering walls of flint rise high in the darkness that holds a rocky ceiling
far above your head. The natural tunnel is filled with debris broken loose from
the crumbling surroundings. A yawning silence lies over the tunnel, yet there is
something, somewhere in the distance, making faint repetitive noises.
name: Broken Passage

desc:
As you glance at the torn stone walls from which deep darkness seems to emanate,
tiny gleaming dots appear like stars slowly taking form behind passing clouds in
the night. As your vision is adjusted, the shining eyes reveal themselves as
reflections in the rock. A big lode of iron ore is located just beneath the
surface of the rock awaiting only a swift stroke of a pickaxe to free it from
the rock that has bound it for ages.
name: Dark Corner

desc:
Passage through the tunnel becomes troublesome as the walls narrow in and the
ceiling descends, bearing closer and closer to the rising floor. The rough walls
gleam in the darkness with a metallic shine indicating rich deposits of iron
ore. A whispering echo of dripping water pervades the silence in the tunnel; the
sound appears to be louder to the east.
name: Low Tunnel

desc:
Nestled within the dark curves of the Dwarven tunnel system, this storage room
provides a little light. Old, rundown tools and supplies litter the shelves on
the walls mixing with other goods. Hanging from the ceiling is a simple torch
which sometimes flickers.
name: A Supply Room within the Mines

desc:
The looming darkness makes it hard to see your surroundings. Black creeping
rocks line the floor making any journey difficult. The natural passage lies deep
beneath the Blue Mountains, where the secret of its presence eludes most regular
visitors. A rough ceiling above can barely be seen in the enshrouding darkness.
name: Dark Passage

desc:
The wiry passage suddenly changes its route digging up through the mountain. The
colour of the rocky walls slowly changes from black to blue as the passage
climbs through different layers of stone. Fresh air comes from a narrow shaft
leading up.
name: Climbing Passage

desc:
The ground rises steeply under a low ceiling. The ceiling is only a few feet
from the rubble-strewn rocky floor that twists upwards silently warning you to
look out for a slide. It is worn with deep cracks, and seems to be only waiting
for a chance to cause a cave-in. Crawling either up or down the tilting ground
will require great effort.
name: Crumbling Incline

desc:
The slanting tunnel begins to narrow in, and gradually light fades as the dark
rock of the tunnel walls seems to absorb every colour. Moving through the tunnel
would be easy, yet many cobwebs hanging from the ceiling are a constant nuisance
as they cling to your head. A faint repeating noise can occasionally be heard,
but it always fades quickly.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel slowly makes its way upwards, passing under a low ceiling of dark
rock. In some places the incline is barely noticeable, so the tunnel can be
easily traversed, yet constant dodging would be needed to avoid the old
dust-covered cobwebs that hang from the ceiling. Far away a weak sound of metal
against stone can be heard.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The black rock walls close together, leaving barely enough room to allow
passage. The ruined roof covers the tunnel, pointed ends of splinters directed
at any intruder. Sharp rocks on the ground make movement even more difficult. A
faint sound of mining taking place far off in the mountain can be heard.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
Bleak walls of smooth rock leap upwards in a curving motion bending inwards to
conjoin with the cavern ceiling about sixteen feet above the floor. The cavern
is fairly large and obviously carved with much determination and skill. The
level floor is hard and rings out in alarm with every step you take, the echoes
bouncing from wall to wall all around you.
name: Cavern in the Mines

desc:
The walls of the tunnel bend inwards towards each other slowly transforming into
a crumbling ceiling above. The floor is lined with loose rocks from a recent
collapse. A wooden structure of planks and thick beams has been constructed to
prevent the tunnel from caving in completely. There are no apparent signs of ore
here.
name: Worn Mine Tunnel

desc:
Scars and chips in the sides of the tunnel show obvious signs of heavy mining
over the long years. Now the tunnel walls stand bare and cracked, and knowing
the thoroughness of the dwarves, one should not expect too much from mining
here.
name: Worn Tunnel

desc:
With the skill bestowed in the Dwarves, they picked a tunnel through this region
of loose rock and rubble. The tunnel walls has been fortified with a wooden
structure of strong oak beams that span across the ceiling to keep the tunnel
from caving in. Now the tunnel must be merely a part of the route by which the
Dwarves transport raw materials and various tools and supplies through the mine.
name: Worn Mine Tunnel

desc:
In the vaporous air breathing is troubled by a salty taste that combined with a
fusty smell invokes a nauseating sensation. The rugged floor is slippery and
treacherous, covered by slimy fungi and pale moss and soaked by a never-ending
stream of water beads inching their way down the cold walls. Nowhere is to be
seen even a slightest hint of dwarven activity.
name: Moist Passage

desc:
Water drips from every conceivable irregularity in the stone ceiling and forms
shallow pools on the ground. The air is moist and leaves a slight acidic taste
in your mouth. Various fungi thrive in the wet environment at the base of the
cold stone walls. Some caution is needed to avoid slipping on the wet floor.
name: Moist Passage

desc:
Tiny streams of water flow down the cracks in the moss-covered walls. The air
smells damp and stale, mainly due to a foul stench emanating from the southern
wall. Water is gathering in bleak pools on the rough floor which becomes very
slippery to the south. Supporting the weight of the Blue Mountains, the ceiling
rests heavily on the surrounding walls.
name: Moist Tunnel

desc:
Bleak puddles of stale-smelling water dominate the broken floor of the tunnel.
The sound of water dripping slowly from the tips of rocks protruding from the
ceiling can be heard. Veins of glistening ore run over the tunnel walls yet
there are no signs indicating the tunnel was ever mined.
name: Wet Tunnel

desc:
Movement in the narrow tunnel is difficult, complicated by many rocks protruding
from the ceiling. From the gaps in the cracked stone dim reflections of light
sparkle in the richness of the underground. A pesky odour sweeps across the
rugged stone walls with an intrusive determination.
name: Low Tunnel

desc:
Condensed water streams down the tattered stone walls. Deep cracks and holes in
the flint decorate the whole passage, yet no signs of any Dwarven activity are
to be seen anywhere. In the misty air there is a strange smell, as of rotting
flesh mixed with mouldy fabric; there is no telling where the smell comes from.
name: Damp Passage

desc:
A musty smell fills the moist air in the passage, and the black rock gleams with
a thin layer of condensed water. The water slowly gathers in faint lines and
travels down the walls creating tiny pools in the uneven floor. A crack in the
wall reveals a high concentration of minerals as light is reflected from inside
it with a metallic shine.
name: Damp Passage

desc:
Beneath a low ceiling of sharp rocks runs a low passage through the mountain.
Travel here is treacherous, as the passage twists and turns beneath the ever
impending rocks in the ceiling. A distant sound of metal against hard rock can
be heard as it repeats over and over. The humidity suddenly increases from
barely noticeable until it is quite evident and makes breathing stressful.
name: Low Passage

desc:
Shrouded in darkness, the crumbling passage makes its way through an area of
flint. In every corner the darkness lurks as if hiding something. The passage
seems natural, formed long ago when the mountain was still young.
name: Dark Passage

desc:
Cramped between the rough surfaces of the surrounding stone walls is a tight
space that snakes its way up and down through endless formations of blue rock
that shines with a crystalline glitter as light exposes its surface. Rocky edges
in the stone make passage difficult as they seize anything trying to make its
way through.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
A low passage hews its way through different layers of hard rock. The colour of
the stone shifts from blue to black as the passage continues up and down through
the underground. Cobwebs are plentiful in every corner and crack, and a stale
smell is in the air.
name: Low Passage

desc:
Stooping low beneath a ceiling of fierce rocks, movement becomes difficult as
the tunnel gradually narrows in. The hard rocks shift in colour as the passage
slowly advances, up and down, through the heart of the mountain. The air is
dense and moist, making breathing tiresome.
name: Low Passage
